domain: Sumerian cuneiform clay tablet (reed-stylus wedge impressed into wet clay; fixed wedge-primitive vocabulary; air-dried or kiln-fired to lock inscription permanently)
specific_mechanism: Fixed-primitive impression on receptive substrate followed by fire-locking — once fired the tablet is unmodifiable; pre-firing it is malleable.
llm_application: CUNEIFORM-FINE-TUNE: a constrained fine-tuning protocol — (1) MALLEABLE PHASE: select a fixed small set T = {t_1,…,t_K} of prompt templates (the wedge primitives); (2) IMPRESSION PHASE: apply gradient updates only on data generated from T (no open data); (3) FIRE-LOCK PHASE: freeze the adapter + apply numerical stabilisation (quantise to INT8 + bind weights via cryptographic hash registry); (4) READ-ONLY PHASE: adapter signed and unmodifiable. Differs from standard PEFT/LoRA by restricting training-time prompts to a fixed K-sized template vocabulary AND enforcing post-training numerical freeze + cryptographic seal.
novelty_claim: Standard PEFT/LoRA uses open instruction data. Mongolian airag (R371) continuous federated FT is open-domain. Cherokee syllabary (R372) is INPUT tokenizer fixed-vocabulary. Constrained-Decoding restricts OUTPUT to grammar. CUNEIFORM-FINE-TUNE differs by restricting TRAINING-TIME PROMPT VOCABULARY to a small fixed wedge-set + adding FIRE-LOCK (quantise+freeze+cryptographic sign).
